description_long: This flower displays a striking arrangement of broad, fleshy petals in vivid shades of red, orange, and yellow, with subtle gradients blending into pink and white near the center. The petals are thick and somewhat undulating, radiating outward from a central core, giving the flower a dramatic, almost sculptural appearance. The central part of the flower is bulbous and layered, with inner petals forming a cup-like structure surrounded by larger, more open outer petals. The surface of the petals appears smooth and slightly glossy, with a waxy texture typical of many tropical or ornamental flowers. No stem or leaves are visible, and the flower seems to be presented as a single, isolated bloom. The coloration and petal structure suggest a species adapted for visual attraction, possibly for pollinators in a garden or park setting.
description: A vibrant, sculptural flower with thick, radiating red, orange, and yellow petals and a bulbous, layered center. The petals are smooth, glossy, and arranged in a dramatic, radial pattern.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full, open face of the flower with all petals radiating outward and the central cup-like structure clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the flower's thickness and the layered, fleshy nature of the petals, with the central core appearing more elongated.
description_view_2: Rear view is similar to the front but with subtle differences in petal overlap and less visibility of the central cup.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the symmetry and thickness of the petals, with the central structure appearing elongated and narrow.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, radiating symmetrically from the central core. There is clear overlapping and layering, with inner petals forming a cup-like structure and outer petals spreading outward in a whorled pattern.
